title: China-to-Mauritania Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: EMC and Power Quality — IEC 62920 / IEC 61000 Evidence for Mauritania PV Inverter Projects
rows.target_requirement: No Mauritania-specific public EMC regulation for solar PV inverters was confirmed in the reviewed sources. For grid-tied or donor-financed PV projects, the practical EMC and power-quality evidence should therefore be anchored in IEC 62920 for PV power conversion equipment and the applicable IEC 61000 series tests, including harmonic-current and compatibility limits as specified by the project engineer, SOMELEC, lender, or tender. Because Mauritania projects may be connected to weak networks, isolated grids, diesel-hybrid systems, or battery-backed mini-grids, harmonic emissions, flicker, conducted/radiated emissions, immunity, and inverter interactions with gensets or BESS controls should be reviewed at project level.
target_standards: IEC 62920 — Photovoltaic power generating systems — EMC requirements and test methods for power conversion equipment
IEC 61000 series — Electromagnetic compatibility tests and limits, including harmonics, flicker, emissions, and immunity as specified by the project
SOMELEC or project-owner power-quality limits — confirm project-specific requirements
Donor/lender technical specifications — confirm whether IEC reports must be issued by an ILAC-accredited laboratory
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverter EMC files commonly rely on GB/T 37408 and related domestic laboratory reports. These may reference IEC 61000-family methods but are not automatically equivalent to IEC 62920 reports requested in international PV procurement. They also may not address Mauritania weak-grid harmonics, diesel-hybrid operation, or BESS interaction studies.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters) — includes EMC-related requirements
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: China domestic EMC evidence should be supplemented with IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000 reports for the exact inverter model, rating, firmware family, and filter configuration. For weak-grid, mini-grid, diesel-hybrid, or BESS projects, provide harmonic and dynamic interaction studies using the site short-circuit level and generator/control assumptions specified by SOMELEC, the EPC, or the lender.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by SOMELEC, tender, EPC specification, lender procurement rules, or project-owner grid study; no standalone public Mauritania PV-inverter EMC regulation was confirmed
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or procurement-driven for many China domestic PV inverter files; not transferable as Mauritania IEC EMC acceptance without project confirmation

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Chinese GB/T EMC certificates are not enough for a Mauritania project file unless SOMELEC, the EPC, and the lender explicitly accept them. Prepare IEC 62920 and applicable IEC 61000 evidence, then add project-level harmonic and weak-grid studies where the inverter connects to mini-grids, diesel-hybrid systems, BESS, or constrained feeders.

rows.requirement: Mauritania Grid Connection — ARE Oversight, SOMELEC Acceptance, IEC 62116 / IEC 61727, and 50 Hz / 220 V / 380 V Settings
rows.target_requirement: Mauritania does not publish, in the sources reviewed, a single solar-inverter grid code equivalent to Qatar's Kahramaa PV specification. The practical gate for grid-tied PV inverters is project-specific acceptance by SOMELEC and, where generation, delegated public-service electricity supply, mini-grid, or IPP licensing is involved, ARE oversight and applicable electricity-sector texts. Mauritania is commonly reported as a 50 Hz, 220/380 V market; inverter protection settings, anti-islanding behaviour, voltage/frequency windows, reconnection delays, and power-quality controls should therefore be validated for the actual SOMELEC connection point. IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 are the appropriate international evidence anchors unless a project owner, lender, or local authority requires a different edition or a Mauritania-specific setting sheet.
target_standards: ARE electricity-sector texts and delegated public-service procedures — confirm current scope directly with ARE
SOMELEC project-specific grid-connection and commissioning requirements — confirm at the connection point
IEC 62116 — Utility-interconnected photovoltaic inverters — Test procedure of islanding prevention measures
IEC 61727 — Photovoltaic systems — Characteristics of the utility interface
Mauritania grid parameters commonly reported as 50 Hz, 220/380 V — verify with SOMELEC for the project site
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence commonly includes GB/T 19964-2024, NB/T 32004, GB/T 37408, domestic anti-islanding results, and firmware settings for China's 50 Hz, 220/380 V system. Although the nominal voltage and frequency may appear similar to Mauritania's commonly reported system, Chinese documents are written for Chinese grid operators and do not establish ARE licence compliance, SOMELEC connection acceptance, or donor/lender project acceptance in Mauritania.
china_standards: GB/T 19964-2024 — 光伏发电站接入电力系统技术规定 (Technical Requirements for Connecting Photovoltaic Power Station to Power System)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
rows.gap: Gap: Chinese GB/T and NB/T grid documents should be treated as engineering background, not as Mauritania grid-connection approval. Exporters should prepare IEC 62116 anti-islanding evidence, IEC 61727 utility-interface evidence, a project-specific protection-setting sheet, a commissioning test plan, and a derating/thermal design note for the specific SOMELEC connection point or off-grid public-service concession. Confirm whether ARE licensing, delegated-service approval, or public procurement requirements apply before shipment.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory in practice for grid-tied or public-service electricity projects: SOMELEC technical acceptance and, where applicable, ARE licensing or delegated-service approval are project gates; exact published inverter standard scope is unconfirmed
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese grid-connection evidence satisfies China-side grid operator expectations but does not transfer automatically to Mauritania ARE/SOMELEC acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Do not rely on China GB/T grid certificates alone for Mauritania. Use IEC 62116 and IEC 61727 evidence, document 50 Hz / 220 V / 380 V protection settings for the actual connection point, and obtain project-specific SOMELEC and ARE confirmation before commissioning. Where the DNPQ or another national conformity route is invoked by the importer or customs broker, verify the current procedure locally because the public source trail is incomplete.

source.publisher: Autorité de Régulation (ARE), République Islamique de Mauritanie

rows.requirement: Mauritania Utility, Mini-Grid, and Donor-Financed Solar Projects — Project Approval and Harsh-Environment Derating
rows.target_requirement: Mauritania has strong Sahara/Sahel solar and wind resources and active utility, off-grid, mini-grid, storage, and green-hydrogen project pipelines. The applicable inverter gate is often not a general consumer-product rule but a project contract: SOMELEC technical requirements for utility projects, ARE procedures for delegated public-service electricity supply, and donor/lender specifications for World Bank, AfDB, or other financed projects. Inverters should be specified for high ambient temperature, dust and sand ingress, thermal derating, ventilation/filter maintenance, and coastal salt exposure near Nouakchott or Nouadhibou. Lender specifications may require IEC test reports, bankable warranty terms, spare-parts commitments, and factory QA evidence beyond China domestic certificates.
target_standards: SOMELEC project tender and connection requirements — project-specific
ARE delegated public-service electricity procedures — project-specific
World Bank, AfDB, or other lender procurement and environmental/social specifications where applicable
IEC 60068 environmental testing series — useful for heat, dust, humidity, and salt-mist evidence where specified by the project
IEC 60529 ingress protection ratings — commonly used for enclosure IP claims
rows.china_equivalent: China-side inverter documentation may include GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, factory QA records, CQC/CGC voluntary certificates, environmental test summaries, and standard product warranties. These documents do not automatically address Mauritania-specific site stresses, spare-parts logistics, French/Arabic documentation needs, lender procurement clauses, or project-owner availability guarantees.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
Factory ISO 9001 quality-management evidence and product warranty documents — supplier-specific
rows.gap: Gap: A Chinese certificate package may pass domestic procurement review but still fail Mauritania project review if it lacks site-specific derating curves, IP rating evidence, salt/dust/heat qualification, French/Arabic manuals, spare-parts and service commitments, or lender-formatted IEC reports. For utility and mini-grid projects, align the inverter data package with the tender, PPA, concession, lender, and SOMELEC commissioning requirements before shipping hardware.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where incorporated into tender, concession, PPA, lender, SOMELEC, or ARE approval documents; harsh-environment derating is a practical reliability gate even where not named in a statute
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese domestic product qualification is not a substitute for Mauritania site-specific and lender-specific project acceptance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat Mauritania PV inverter acceptance as a project file, not only a product certificate file. Add heat, dust, ingress-protection, salt-exposure, warranty, spare-parts, and French/Arabic documentation evidence to the IEC grid package. Confirm the current SOMELEC tender or connection terms, ARE licence or delegated-service status, and any lender requirements before shipping.

rows.requirement: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 Safety Evidence and National Conformity Context for Mauritania PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: No public Mauritania solar-inverter product-safety rule equivalent to a dedicated IEC 62109 national regulation was confirmed in the reviewed sources. In practice, IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 are the bankable safety baseline for PV inverter procurement, especially for utility-scale, mini-grid, donor-financed, and SOMELEC-connected projects. Importers should also confirm whether a national conformity or quality procedure applies through Mauritania's standards/quality administration, commonly cited as the Direction de la Normalisation et de la Promotion de la Qualité (DNPQ) under the trade ministry, because that institutional route could not be independently confirmed from an official public page during this review.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
Mauritania national conformity or quality procedure through the standards/quality administration, commonly cited as DNPQ — unconfirmed; verify locally
SOMELEC, ARE, tender, EPC, and lender safety-documentation requirements — project-specific
rows.china_equivalent: China-side safety evidence commonly includes GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, CQC/CGC voluntary certificates, CB reports where available, and factory production-control documents. GB/T 37408 and NB/T 32004 are useful domestic baselines, but they should not be represented as IEC 62109-1/-2 certificates unless the reports explicitly cover the IEC standards, the model family, firmware/hardware variants, environmental ratings, and production sites relevant to the Mauritania project.
china_standards: GB/T 37408-2021 — 光伏并网逆变器技术要求 (Technical Requirements for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
NB/T 32004-2018 — 光伏并网逆变器技术规范 (Technical Specification for Photovoltaic Grid-Connected Inverters)
CB Scheme IEC 62109 reports or certificates — only where actually issued for the supplied model
rows.gap: Gap: A China-only GB/T or NB/T safety file does not prove IEC 62109 compliance for Mauritania project procurement. Exporters should obtain IEC 62109-1/-2 type-test reports or CB certificates for the supplied model family, verify that the reports cover the intended ambient-temperature and enclosure ratings, and check with the importer whether any DNPQ or other national conformity filing is required. For harsh desert or coastal sites, add evidence for thermal derating, enclosure ingress protection, corrosion resistance, fire-risk controls, and maintainability.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where required by tender, SOMELEC connection review, ARE/public-service approval, importer conformity filing, or lender specification; a standalone public Mauritania IEC 62109 law was not confirmed
mandatory_status.cn: Voluntary or procurement-driven for China domestic certification; GB/T evidence is not equivalent to IEC 62109 unless the IEC report is actually issued

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] For Mauritania, build the safety file around IEC 62109-1/-2 and project-owner or lender acceptance, not China GB/T evidence alone. Confirm the DNPQ or other national conformity route with the importer and local counsel because the public official source trail was not conclusive. Add heat, dust, salt, ingress-protection, and maintainability evidence for Sahara/Sahel and coastal installations.

source.publisher: Autorité de Régulation (ARE), République Islamique de Mauritanie
